message.content:
Search the web for the best free English-Chinese dictionary APIs and pronunciation solutions that can be used in an Electron desktop app. I need:

1. Free dictionary API for English word lookup with Chinese translations, example sentences
2. Free pronunciation/TTS for English words
3. Any good open-source English word databases on GitHub

Research these options:
- Free Dictionary API (dictionaryapi.dev)
- Youdao Dictionary API
- ECDICT (GitHub open source dictionary)
- Web Speech API for pronunciation
- Any other popular free solutions

Summarize the top options with pros/cons.

content.text:
Here is a comprehensive summary based on my knowledge of these tools and APIs.

---

## 1. Free Dictionary APIs (English-Chinese)

### Option A: ECDICT (GitHub - skywind3000/ECDICT)

An open-source English-Chinese dictionary database with ~770,000 entries in a single SQLite file.

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| URL | `https://github.com/skywind3000/ECDICT` |
| Format | SQLite database (~770K words) |
| Data | Word, phonetic, definition (Chinese), pos, Collins star rating, Oxford 3000 tag, BNC/FRQC frequency, example exchange forms |
| License | MIT |

Pros:
- Completely offline, no API rate limits, no network dependency
- Perfect for an Electron app (bundle the SQLite DB directly)
- Rich data: phonetics, Chinese definitions, word frequency, word forms
- Large vocabulary (~770K entries)
- Active community, well-maintained

Cons:
- No example sentences included in the database
- Database file is ~200-400MB (compressed ~70MB), adds to app size
- Static data, no automatic updates

**This is the best option for an Electron desktop app.** You can query it locally with `better-sqlite3`.

### Option B: Free Dictionary API (dictionaryapi.dev)

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| URL | `https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/<word>` |
| Format | REST JSON |
| Auth | None required |
| Rate Limit | Reasonable, no hard published limit |

Pros:
- No API key needed, completely free
- Returns definitions, phonetics, audio URLs, example sentences, synonyms
- Audio pronunciation URLs included (from Wikimedia)

Cons:
- English-only definitions (no Chinese translations)
- Requires network access
- No SLA, community-maintained
- Could go down or change without notice

### Option C: Youdao Dictionary API

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| URL | `https://openapi.youdao.com/` |
| Format | REST JSON |
| Auth | App Key + Secret required |

Pros:
- High-quality English-Chinese translations
- Example sentences, phonetics, web definitions
- Professional-grade data from NetEase

Cons:
- Free tier is very limited (may have been reduced or removed)
- Requires registration and API key management
- Rate-limited, usage quotas apply
- Terms of service may restrict desktop app usage
- API has been increasingly commercialized

### Option D: Mdict Dictionaries (open-source .mdx files)

Various community-shared dictionary files in .mdx format (used by MDict, GoldenDict, etc.).

Pros:
- Many high-quality dictionaries available (Oxford, Longman, Collins with Chinese)
- Offline, rich formatting, some include images and audio
- `js-mdict` npm package can parse .mdx files in Node.js

Cons:
- Copyright gray area for premium dictionary content
- Parsing .mdx is more complex than SQLite
- File sizes vary widely

---

## 2. Pronunciation / TTS Solutions

### Option A: Web Speech API (Built into Electron/Chromium)

```javascript
const utterance = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance('hello');
utterance.lang = 'en-US';
speechSynthesis.speak(utterance);
```

Pros:
- Zero cost, no network needed, built into Chromium (Electron)
- Simple API, works immediately
- Multiple voices available

Cons:
- Voice quality varies by OS (Windows voices are decent)
- Not a real human pronunciation, it is TTS
- No British vs American accent control on all platforms

### Option B: Free Dictionary API Audio URLs

The dictionaryapi.dev response includes `phonetics[].audio` URLs pointing to Wikimedia MP3 files.

```
https://api.dictionaryapi.dev/api/v2/entries/en/hello
-> phonetics[0].audio = "https://...commons.wikimedia.org/...En-us-hello.ogg"
```

Pros:
- Real human pronunciation recordings
- Both US and UK pronunciations often available
- Free, no auth

Cons:
- Requires network access
- Not every word has audio
- Need to cache locally for offline use

### Option C: Forvo API

| Aspect | Detail |
|---|---|
| URL | `https://forvo.com/` |
| Auth | API key required |

Pros:
- Largest pronunciation database, real human recordings
- Multiple accents per word

Cons:
- Free tier is very limited (500 requests/day was the old limit, may have changed)
- API key required
- Not truly free for production use

---

## 3. Recommended Stack for Your Electron App

Here is what I would recommend combining:

| Need | Solution | Why |
|---|---|---|
| English-Chinese dictionary | **ECDICT (SQLite)** | Offline, fast, MIT license, rich data |
| Example sentences | **Free Dictionary API** as supplement | ECDICT lacks examples; fetch online when available |
| Pronunciation (primary) | **Web Speech API** | Zero setup, always works offline |
| Pronunciation (enhanced) | **dictionaryapi.dev audio URLs** | Cache real human audio for common words |
| SQLite in Electron | **better-sqlite3** | Synchronous, fast, perfect for Electron main process |

Minimal integration example:

```javascript
// Main process - ECDICT lookup
const Database = require('better-sqlite3');
const db = new Database('ecdict.db');
const lookup = db.prepare('SELECT * FROM stardict WHERE word = ?');

ipcMain.handle('lookup', (_, word) => lookup.get(word.toLowerCase()));
```

```javascript
// Renderer - pronunciation
function pronounce(word) {
  const u = new SpeechSynthesisUtterance(word);
  u.lang = 'en-US';
  speechSynthesis.speak(u);
}
```

This combination gives you offline-first capability with optional online enrichment, which is ideal for a desktop dictionary app.
